summary:
When both parents have achondroplasia, there is a 25% chance their child will have normal stature due to inheriting two recessive alleles—one from each parent—consistent with autosomal dominant inheritance patterns. This result is confirmed by direct statement in source [7].  

The summary above contains only essential information related to the query about probabilities of unaffected babies under given parental conditions; it avoids extraneous details such as mortality risks associated with homozygosity which were mentioned but aren't central here.

---

Final Answer:

\boxed{B}

We are now ready to finalize the response.
